Hell..Back: These muscles keep shortening, as the disease progresses, just as they do in cerebro-spinal meningitis; until the head is drawn back as far as it can go.
Hell..Bladder: The urine is retained or suppressed; sometimes it dribbles away passes unconsciously. Urine passed in a feeble stream; bloody urine.
Hell..Chill: Rarely much disturbed by being touched, or by being covered too warmly, or by not being covered at all. He does not seem to be sensitive to heat, or cold, or pricking, or handling or pinching.
Hell..Extremities: The patient lies on the back, with his limbs drawn up; or slides down in bed. Great debility; great relaxation; the muscles refuse to act.
Hell..Generalities: The Helleborus patient is benumbed everywhere. The whole sensorium is in a benumbed state, a stupefaction, a blunting of general sensibility.
Hell..Head: Burning heat in the head; shooting pains; pressive pains in the head from congestion. Violent occipital headache. Dull aching in the occiput; benumbed feeling in the occiput. A feeling like wood fullness, congestion and pressure.
Hell..Mind: In all the complaints of Helleborus stupefaction occurs in greater or less degree. Sometimes it is a complete stupor, sometimes a partial stupor, but it is always stupefaction and sluggishness.
Hell..Mind, quiet, quiet disposition: Delirium is not common, and when present it is muttering. There is more stupefaction, more "do nothing," more "say nothing," than delirium.
Hell..Mouth: The patient lies upon the back, eyes partly open, rolling the head, mouth open, tongue dry, eyes lusterless, staring into space.
Hell..Rectum: In the early part of the proving there are diarrhoea and dysentery; with copious white gelatinous stool; stool consisting solely of pale tenacious mucus. And then comes paralytic constipation.
Hell..Skin: It is not so much the awful pains, but it is the itching, tingling and formication that cause the appearance of extreme agony. Sometimes in every part of the child's body it is a week before all these symptoms go away of themselves but they will go away, if left alone.
Hell..Stomach: The nausea and vomiting are nondescript.
Hell..Appetite, increased (hunger in general): There is violent thirst in these fevers, and unusual canine hunger.
Hell..Stomach, thirst: There is violent thirst in these fevers, and unusual canine hunger.
Hell..Bladder, urination: The urine is retained or suppressed; sometimes it dribbles away passes unconsciously. Urine passed in a feeble stream; bloody urine.
Hell..Generalities, pain: Burning heat in the head; shooting pains; pressive pains in the head from congestion.
Hell..Generalities, weakness: Great debility; great relaxation; the muscles refuse to act.
Hell..Head, congestion: The headaches, the motions of the head and the appearance of the face are those occurring in congestion of the brain.
Hell..Mind, quiet: Listlessness. What is called in the text "stubborn silence" is more an apathetic silence, an inability to speak. It appears as if he refused to answer, but he does not; he does not know how to answer; he cannot think.
Hell..Appetite: There is violent thirst in these fevers, and unusual canine hunger.
Hell..Clinical: It is a clinical observation which you will see if you see Hellebore cases, and Zincum cases. Zincum is, if possible, even more profound in its dreadful state of stupefaction than Hellebore.
Hell..Abdomen: In the early part of the proving there are diarrhoea and dysentery; with copious white gelatinous stool; stool consisting solely of pale tenacious mucus.
